user_message: Just came out of whitespace analysis with Pacific Northwest Outfitters. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-12 13:32 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Pacific Northwest Outfitters whitespace analysis

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about whitespace analysis. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 11:40 EST
Subject: Re: Re: whitespace analysis Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with whitespace analysis. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Pacific Northwest Outfitters: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-M trap (No native 'cross-sell signal' primitive distinct from generic signal-to-opportun); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-M failure mode (No native 'cross-sell signal' primitive distinct from generic signal-to-opportun)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-M -- No native 'cross-sell signal' primitive distinct from generic signal-to-opportun
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
